text.title: LB High Availability Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for HA load-balanced topologies. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Any production service with SLA ≥ 99.9% MUST front itself with ≥ 2 LB instances in active-active or active-passive configuration. A single LB instance is not HA regardless of how many backends sit behind it.

rationale.source: AWS Well-Architected + Cloud LB HA references

rule.rationale: A single LB instance is itself a SPOF — process crash, host failure, or network partition takes the whole service down. ≥ 2 instances + automatic failover (VRRP / managed LB pair) is the minimum that satisfies N-1.

rule.statement: Backend instances MUST be distributed across ≥ 2 availability zones. Each AZ MUST host ≥ 2 healthy backend instances (i.e., total ≥ 4 backends for N-1 in each AZ).
rule.rationale: A single-AZ backend pool means an AZ outage takes the whole service down — the LB is healthy but every backend is unreachable. ≥ 2 AZs with ≥ 2 backends each survives one full AZ failure with capacity.

rule.statement: For multi-region HA with SLA ≥ 99.99%, the global LB MUST be anycast (AWS Global Accelerator, GCP Global HTTP(S) LB, Cloudflare LB). DNS-based GSLB (Route 53 latency / geo routing, NS1, Akamai GTM) is forbidden at this SLA tier.

rationale.source: AWS Global Accelerator FAQ + Cloudflare LB docs

rule.rationale: DNS-based GSLB failover is 30 s – 5 min in practice (resolver caches ignore short TTLs); anycast failover is < 30 s at the BGP layer.

rule.statement: Zero-downtime deployments MUST implement: (a) SIGTERM handler that flips readiness probe to 503 immediately; (b) deregistration delay / drain timeout ≥ 30 s; (c) process exit only after drain completes. Plain `kill -TERM` followed by immediate exit is forbidden.

rationale.source: Kubernetes graceful-shutdown docs + LB drain references

rule.rationale: Without readiness flip on SIGTERM, the LB keeps sending new requests for the next 10–30 s; the dying process answers with 502/connection-reset. Drain delay lets in-flight requests complete cleanly.

rule.statement: Multi-AZ LBs (e.g., AWS ALB with subnets in 3 AZs) MUST NOT be paired with single-AZ backend pools. The LB target group MUST have healthy targets in every subnet AZ the LB listens on.
rule.rationale: A multi-AZ LB with single-AZ backends still goes fully down if that one AZ fails; it merely looks HA in the dashboard.
